data.title: Japan Prepared to Intervene in Currency Market Amid Speculation, Official Says
data.description: Japan's Finance Ministry official, Atsushi Mimura, stated on Friday that speculative activities in the market continue, indicating Japan's readiness to intervene to support the yen. According to Jin10, when asked about potential intervention by Tokyo in the currency market, Mimura said, "I do not intend to comment on the actions we will take next. But I want to tell you that Japan's Golden Week holiday has just begun." Mimura declined to comment on whether Japan intervened in the foreign exchange market on Thursday. When questioned about whether exchange rate fluctuations are still considered speculative, Mimura responded, "My view has not changed." Analysts note that due to the Golden Week holiday, Japan's stock market will be closed from Monday to Wednesday next week. This could lead to significant yen volatility due to insufficient market liquidity.

data.title: Stablecoin Payments Could Transform Financial Infrastructure, Says Stanford Professor
data.description:
Stanford Professor Ruizhe Jia highlighted the potential impact of stablecoin-based payments during the EASYResidency workshop. YZi Labs posted on X. Jia emphasized that stablecoins could significantly alter financial infrastructure, particularly in fragmented markets. He noted that the competition to dominate the payment sector is intensifying, with the development of innovative applications being crucial.

Jia pointed to Alipay and WeChat Pay as examples of how controlling the payment layer can lead to becoming a central hub for various financial services, including savings, lending, and investing. He suggested that stablecoins might represent the next evolution of this model in many regions, although the market is still in its early stages.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Demis Hassabis Discusses AGI Timeline and Entrepreneurial Advice
data.description: Demis Hassabis, a prominent figure in artificial intelligence, shared insights on the timeline for achieving Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), suggesting that it could be realized by 2030. According to PANews, Hassabis emphasized the importance for deep tech entrepreneurs to prepare in advance for this significant development. He also reflected on the potential for scientific breakthroughs following AlphaFold and addressed challenges related to reasoning flaws and memory limitations. The podcast encapsulates these key points, offering valuable advice for those in the field.

data.title: X Platform Introduces Cashtags Feature for Real-Time Asset Tracking
data.description: X Platform has launched the Cashtags feature on its web interface, offering real-time charts and posts for each asset, positioning itself as a key component of user trading terminals. According to PANews, this follows the earlier introduction of the Cashtags feature on iPhone devices in the United States and Canada, providing users with real-time financial data.

data.title: Wasabi Protocol Confirms Solana Contracts Unaffected by Recent Vulnerability
data.description: Wasabi Protocol has announced that its contracts on the Solana blockchain remain secure and are not impacted by the recent vulnerability affecting its EVM deployment. According to ChainCatcher, the issue is confined to Wasabi's Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) deployment. The team is collaborating with top security experts and has reached out to law enforcement agencies, including the FBI. Further updates will be provided as they become available.

data.title: Tesla Reports Over $500 Million Revenue from SpaceX and xAI Sales
data.description: Tesla updated its report on Thursday, revealing that it generated over $500 million in revenue through product sales to SpaceX and xAI. According to Jin10, Tesla had previously disclosed in January that its business dealings with xAI amounted to approximately $430.1 million. The updated report on Thursday indicates that Tesla earned an additional $143.3 million from SpaceX.

data.title: India Faces Intense Heatwave Amid Energy Shortages
data.description: India is expected to experience a severe heatwave until the monsoon rains arrive in June, Bloomberg posted on X. The country is already facing higher-than-average temperatures, which are putting additional pressure on power grids during a period of energy shortages. The situation is exacerbated by the increased demand for electricity as people seek relief from the heat. Authorities are closely monitoring the situation and are urging citizens to conserve energy wherever possible. The upcoming monsoon season is anticipated to bring some relief, but until then, the nation must contend with the challenges posed by the extreme weather conditions.

data.title: Polymarket's Pregnancy Market Faces Dispute Over Resolution
data.description: Polymarket's market titled 'Clavicular pregnancy in 2026?' has seen a lifetime trading volume of $16.46 million and is currently embroiled in a dispute on UMA. According to NS3.AI, traders are contesting the resolution of the contract. On April 30, 'Yes' shares were trading at 97 cents, despite market rules stating that non-credible pregnancy announcements are not valid. Since April 20, the market has experienced two cycles of proposals and disputes. A trader named Domahhhh criticized UMA, labeling it a 'disinformation engine.' Meanwhile, Polymarket has expressed interest in managing the resolution process internally, although no timeline has been established for this transition.

data.title: Japan's Golden Week Holiday Season Begins, Says Finance Ministry Official
data.description: Japan's Finance Ministry official, Atsushi Mimura, announced that the country's Golden Week holiday season has just commenced. According to Jin10, this period is traditionally marked by a series of national holidays, offering many Japanese citizens the opportunity to travel and engage in leisure activities. The Golden Week is one of the busiest holiday seasons in Japan, often leading to increased domestic travel and consumer spending.

data.title: U.S. Crypto Market Structure Bill Gains Momentum in Senate
data.description: The U.S. crypto market structure bill is gaining traction in the Senate Banking Committee, with a hearing aimed for mid-May. According to PANews, unresolved issues remain, such as stablecoin yields, ethical clauses, and U.S. President Donald Trump's crypto interests. Senator Thom Tillis has urged Committee Chair Tim Scott to advance the hearing but will oppose the bill if it lacks ethical provisions. Senator Angela Alsobrooks stressed the need to address illegal finance and ethics for bipartisan support. Senator John Kennedy is withholding support due to housing bill disputes. Chair Scott indicated the bill is in the 'red zone' and hopes for bipartisan review in May, with a Senate vote in June or July.

data.title: South Korea's April Export Growth Revised to 49.2%
data.description: South Korea's export growth for April was revised to 49.2%, surpassing the previous estimate of 48.3%, according to Jin10. The actual growth rate exceeded expectations, which were set at 45.3%. This adjustment reflects a stronger performance in the country's export sector for the month.

data.title: Telegram's Underground Guarantee Market Develops into Integrated Service System
data.description: Telegram's underground guarantee market has evolved into a comprehensive service system, incorporating guarantee matching, fund settlement, merchant management, and traffic distribution. According to PANews, this market is showing clear signs of platformization and networking development.

data.title: Japan's Core Inflation Rate Remains Below Target for Third Consecutive Month
data.description: On May 1, Jin10 reported that Tokyo's core inflation rate stood at 1.5% in April, marking the third consecutive month it remained below the Bank of Japan's target level. This follows a 1.7% increase in March. According to Jin10, the lower inflation rate was attributed to fuel subsidies offsetting the rising raw material costs due to Middle Eastern conflicts. Analysts anticipate that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) will accelerate in the coming months as oil prices surge and the yen weakens, leading to higher import costs. This situation is putting pressure on the Bank of Japan to consider raising interest rates. Earlier this week, the Bank of Japan maintained its benchmark interest rate at 0.75% but acknowledged the need to prevent further increases in potential inflation rates. The bank also pledged to seek further rate hikes once geopolitical risks subside.

data.title: Economists Expect Australian Central Bank to Raise Cash Rate
data.description: A Reuters survey reveals that 33 out of 30 economists anticipate the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) will increase the cash rate to 4.35% on May 5. According to Jin10, more than one-third of the economists expect the RBA to raise the rate to 4.60% or higher this year, a forecast not seen in the March survey.

data.title: Foreign Investment in Japanese Stocks Reaches 807.9 Billion Yen for the Week Ending April 24
data.description: Foreign investors purchased Japanese stocks worth 807.9 billion yen in the week ending April 24. According to Jin10, the previous value was revised from 2.3809 trillion yen to 2.3806 trillion yen.

data.title: Tokyo's April CPI Growth Slows to 1.5%
data.description: Tokyo's Consumer Price Index (CPI) for April recorded a year-on-year increase of 1.5%, according to Jin10. This figure fell short of the anticipated 1.7% and was slightly higher than the previous month's rate of 1.4%.

data.title: Elon Musk's Tesla Compensation Reaches $158 Billion
data.description: Elon Musk's initial compensation figure following the approval of his ambitious pay package by Tesla Inc. shareholders has been revealed, amounting to $158 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the substantial nature of this compensation package, which reflects Musk's significant role and achievements within the company. The package, approved by shareholders, underscores the confidence placed in Musk's leadership and vision for Tesla's future growth and innovation.

data.title: ANZ's First-Half Profit Exceeds Expectations Amid CEO's Turnaround Strategy
data.description: ANZ has reported a first-half profit that exceeded analyst expectations, marking a positive outcome as the new CEO implements a turnaround strategy. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's performance as a significant achievement in the current financial landscape. The results reflect the effectiveness of the strategic changes introduced by the CEO, aiming to enhance the bank's operational efficiency and market position. This development is part of a broader effort to revitalize ANZ's business operations and improve shareholder value.

data.title: Benchmark Analyst Defends Strategy's Preferred Stock Structure Amid Bitcoin Purchases
data.description: Benchmark analyst Mark Palmer has expressed support for Strategy's preferred stock structure, which successfully raised approximately $3.5 billion in the first three weeks of April. According to NS3.AI, these funds were utilized to facilitate Bitcoin acquisitions. Strategy executed three consecutive weekly purchases, accumulating a total of 51,364 BTC, bringing its total holdings to 818,334 BTC. Grayscale's Zach Pandl noted that instruments like STRC are reliant on sustained Bitcoin price increases and possess a risk profile akin to high-yield corporate debt.

data.title: USDT Wallet Analysis Reveals Concentration in Large Transactions
data.description: Everdawn Labs has discovered that a significant majority of USDT0 wallet addresses, specifically 99.2%, hold less than $1,000. According to NS3.AI, the report further highlights that while transfers exceeding $1 million constituted only 1.8% of cross-chain transactions over a recent seven-day period, they represented a substantial 68.8% of the total transaction volume. This indicates a concentration of large transactions within the USDT0 ecosystem.
